message: I've been digging into how to actually *plan* for emergencies with my family, and honestly, having a simple written plan makes such a difference—it takes the guesswork out when things feel chaotic. Turns out the folks at Ready.gov have put together a pretty straightforward way to walk through it together. Nothing overwhelming, just the basics that actually matter. Have you and your people talked through what you'd do if you had to leave home quickly, or where you'd meet up?
